ListItem.Para: Center hydraulic system electric motor-driven pumps operate normally.
Slat secondary mode is verified to operate normally once each flight day.
Trailing edge variable camber (cruise flaps) function is considered inoperative.
Main landing gear priority valve is verified to operate normally once each flight day.

Note.Para: The airplane must also be dispatched using the appropriate sub-item of MEL 27-51-02.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Verify slat secondary mode and main landing gear priority valve operate normally once each flight day (AMM DDG 27-51-03).

ListItem.Para: Do the ground test of the slat secondary mode.
Conduct the ground system test Slats (Electric).
On the expanded flap and slat position indications, confirm leading edge extension to the mid position, then retraction to UP.
After completion of the test, confirm the SLATS SECONDARY FAIL status message is not displayed.
Verify main landing gear priority valve operates normally once each flight day.
With engines not operating, pressurize the center hydraulic system.
Verify that the main landing gear pressure shown on the landing gear maintenance page is more than 4500 psi.
Verify that the main landing gear pressure shown on the landing gear maintenance page is more than 2000 psi.
